author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XXI. 12–19.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With this third manifestation of Himself by the Lord to His disciples after His resurrection, the Gospel of the blessed Apostle John is brought to a close, of which we have already lectured through the earlier part as we were able, on to the place where it is related that an hundred and fifty-three fishes were taken by the disciples to whom He showed Himself, and for all they were so large, yet were not the nets broken.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What follows we have now to take into consideration, and to discuss as the Lord enables us, and as the various points may appear to demand.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the fishing was over, “Jesus saith unto them, Come [and] dine.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And none of those who sat down dared to ask Him, Who art Thou? knowing that it was the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, they knew, what need was there to ask? and if there was no need, wherefore is it said, “they dared not,” as if there were need, but, from some fear or other, they dared not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The meaning here, therefore, is: so great was the evidence of the truth that Jesus Himself had appeared to these disciples, that not one of them dared not merely to deny, but even to doubt it; for had any of them doubted it, he ought certainly to have asked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In this sense, therefore, it was said, “No one dared to ask Him, Who art Thou?” as if it were, No one dared to doubt that it was He Himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And Jesus cometh, and taketh bread, and giveth them, and fish likewise.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We are likewise told here, you see, on what they dined; and of this dinner we also will say something that is sweet and salutary, if we, too, are made by Him to partake of the food.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is related above that these disciples, when they came to the land, “saw a fire of coals laid, and a fish laid thereon, and bread.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here we are not to understand that the bread also was laid upon the coals, but only to supply, They saw.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And if we repeat this verb in the place where it ought to be supplied, the whole may read thus: They saw coals laid, and fish laid thereon, and they saw bread.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or rather in this way: They saw coals laid, and fish laid thereon; they saw also bread.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: At the Lord’s command they likewise brought of the fishes which they themselves had caught; and although their doing so might not be actually stated by the historian, yet there has been no silence in regard to the Lord’s command.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He says, “Bring of the fishes which ye have now caught.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And when we have such certainty that He gave the order, will any suppose that they failed to obey it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of this, therefore, the Lord prepared the dinner for these His seven disciples, namely, of the fish which they had seen laid upon the coals, with an addition thereto from those which they had caught, and of the bread which we are told with equal distinctness that they had seen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The fish roasted is Christ having suffered; He Himself also is the bread that cometh down from heaven.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With Him is incorporated the Church, in order to the participation in everlasting blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this reason is it said, “Bring of the fish which ye have now caught,” that all of us who cherish this hope may know that we ourselves, through that septenary number of disciples whereby our universal community may in this passage be understood as symbolized, partake in this great sacrament, and are associated in the same blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This is the Lord’s dinner with His own disciples, and herewith John, although having much besides that he might say of Christ, brings his Gospel, with profound thought and an eye to important lessons, to a close.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For here the Church, such as it will be hereafter among the good alone, is signified by the draught of an hundred and fifty-three fishes; and to those who so believe, and hope, and love, there is demonstrated by this dinner their participation in such super-eminent blessedness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “This was now,” he says, “the third time that Jesus showed Himself to His disciples after that He was risen from the dead.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this we are to refer not to the manifestations themselves, but to the days (that is to say, taking the first day when He rose again, and the [second] eight days after, when the disciple Thomas saw and believed, and [the third] on this day when He so acted in connection with the fishes, although how many days afterwards it was that He did so we are not told); for on that first day He was seen more than once, as is shown by the collated testimonies of all the evangelists: but, as we have said, it is in accordance with the days that His manifestations are to be calculated, making this the third; for that [manifestation] is to be reckoned the first, and all one and the same, as included in one day, however often and to however many He showed Himself on the day of His resurrection; the second eight days afterwards, and this the third, and thereafter as often as He pleased on to the fortieth day, when He ascended into heaven, although all of them have not been recorded in Scripture.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “So when they had dined, He saith to Simon Peter, Simon, [son] of John, lovest thou me more than these?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto Him, Yea, Lord; Thou knowest that I love Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto him, Feed my lambs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith to him again, Simon, [son] of John, lovest thou me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto Him, Yea, Lord; Thou knowest that I love Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto Him, Feed my lambs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto him the third time, Simon, [son] of John, lovest thou me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter was grieved because He said unto him the third time, Lovest thou me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And he said unto Him, Lord, Thou knowest all things; Thou knowest that I love Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He saith unto him, Feed my sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, When thou wast young thou girdedst thyself, and walkedst whither thou wouldest: but when thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wilt not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this spake He, signifying by what death he should glorify God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such was the end reached by that denier and lover; elated by his presumption, prostrated by his denial, cleansed by his weeping, approved by his confession, crowned by his suffering, this was the end he reached, to die with a perfected love for the name of Him with whom, by a perverted forwardness, he had promised to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He would do, when strengthened by His resurrection, what in his weakness he promised prematurely.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the needful order was that Christ should first die for Peter’s salvation, and then that Peter should die for the preaching of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The boldness thus begun by human temerity was an utter inversion of the order that had been instituted by the Truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Peter thought to lay down his life for Christ, the one to be delivered in behalf of the Deliverer, seeing that Christ had come to lay down His life for all His own, including Peter also, which, you see, was now done.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now and henceforth a true, because graciously bestowed, strength of heart may be assumed for incurring death itself for the name of the Lord, and not a false one presumptuously usurped through an erroneous estimate of ourselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now there is no need that we should any more fear the passage out of the present life, because in the Lord’s resurrection we have a foregoing illustration of the life to come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now thou hast cause, Peter, to be no longer afraid of death, because He liveth whom thou didst mourn when dead, and whom in thy carnal love thou didst try to hinder from dying in our behalf.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou didst dare to step in before the Leader, and thou didst tremble before His persecutor: now that the price has been paid for thee, it is thy duty to follow the Buyer, and follow Him even to the death of the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast heard the words of Him whom thou hast already proved to be truthful; He Himself hath foretold thy suffering, who formerly foretold thy denial.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But first the Lord asks what He knew, and that not once, but a second and a third time, whether Peter loved Him; and just as often He has the same answer, that He is loved, while just as often He gives Peter the same charge to feed His sheep.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To the threefold denial there is now appended a threefold confession, that his tongue may not yield a feebler service to love than to fear, and imminent death may not appear to have elicited more from the lips than present life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let it be the office of love to feed the Lord’s flock, if it was the signal of fear to deny the Shepherd.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who have this purpose in feeding the flock of Christ, that they may have them as their own, and not as Christ’s, are convicted of loving themselves, and not Christ, from the desire either of boasting, or wielding power, or acquiring gain, and not from the love of obeying, serving, and pleasing God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Against such, therefore, there stands as a wakeful sentinel this thrice inculcated utterance of Christ, of whom the apostle complains that they seek their own, not the things that are Jesus Christ’s.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what else mean the words, “Lovest thou me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Feed my sheep,” than if it were said, If thou lovest me, think not of feeding thyself, but feed my sheep as mine, and not as thine own; seek my glory in them, and not thine own; my dominion, and not thine; my gain, and not thine; lest thou be found in the fellowship of those who belong to the perilous times, lovers of their own selves, and all else that is joined on to this beginning of evils?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the apostle, after saying, “For men shall be lovers of their own selves,” proceeded to add, “Lovers of money, boastful, proud, blasphemers, disobedient to parents, unthankful, wicked, irreligious, without affection, false accusers, incontinent, implacable, with out kindness, traitors, heady, blinded; lovers of pleasures more than of God; having a form of godliness, but denying the power thereof.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these evils flow from that as their fountain which he stated first, “lovers of their own selves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: With great propriety, therefore, is Peter addressed, “Lovest thou me?” and found replying, “I love Thee:” and the command applied to him, “Feed my lambs,” and this a second and a third time.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have it also demonstrated here that love and liking are one and the same thing; for the Lord also in the last question said not Diligis me? but, Amas me?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us, then, love not ourselves, but Him; and in feeding His sheep, let us be seeking the things which are His, not the things which are our own.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in some inexplicable way, I know not what, every one that loveth himself, and not God, loveth not himself; and whoever loveth God, and not himself, he it is that loveth himself.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he that cannot live by himself will certainly die by loving himself; he therefore loveth not himself who loves himself to his own loss of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He is loved by whom life is preserved, a man by not loving himself only loveth the more, when it is for this reason that he loveth not himself [namely] that he may love Him by whom he lives.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let not those, then, who feed Christ’s sheep be “lovers of their own selves,” lest they feed them as if they were their own, and not His, and wish to make their own gain of them, as “lovers of money;” or to domineer over them, as “boastful;” or to glory in the honors which they receive at their hands, as “proud;” or to go the length even of originating heresies, as “blasphemers;” and not to give place to the holy fathers, as those who are “disobedient to parents;” and to render evil for good to those who wish to correct them, because unwilling to let them perish, as “unthankful;” to slay their own souls and those of others, as “wicked;” to outrage the motherly bowels of the Church, as “irreligious;” to have no sympathy with the weak, as those who are “without affection;” to attempt to traduce the character of the saints, as “false accusers;” to give loose reins to the basest lusts, as “incontinent;” to make lawsuits their practice, as “implacable;” to know nothing of loving service, as those who are “without kindness;” to make known to the enemies of the godly what they are well aware ought to be kept secret, as “traitors;” to disturb human modesty by shameless discussions, as “heady;” to understand neither what they say nor whereof they affirm, as “blinded;” and to prefer carnal delights to spiritual joys, as those who are “lovers of pleasures more than lovers of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these and such like vices, whether all of them meet in a single individual, or whether some dominate in one and others in another, spring up in some form or another from this one root, when men are “lovers of their own selves.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A vice which is specially to be guarded against by those who feed Christ’s sheep, lest they be seeking their own, not the things that are Jesus Christ’s, and be turning those to the use of their own lusts for whom the blood of Christ was shed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whose love ought, in one who feedeth His sheep, to grow up unto so great a spiritual fervor as to overcome even the natural fear of death, that makes us unwilling to die even when we wish to live with Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Apostle Paul also says that he had a desire to be dissolved, and to be with Christ, and yet he groans, being burdened, and wishes not to be unclothed, but clothed upon, that mortality may be swallowed up of life.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so to His present lover the Lord said, “When thou shalt be old, thou shalt stretch forth thy hands, and another shall gird thee, and carry thee whither thou wouldest not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this He said to him, signifying by what death he should glorify God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Thou shalt stretch forth thy hands,” He said; in other words, thou shalt be crucified.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that thou mayest come to this, “another shall gird thee, and carry thee,” not whither thou wouldest, but “whither thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He told him first what would happen, and then how it should come to pass.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it was not after being crucified, but when actually about to be crucified, that he was carried whither he would not; for after being crucified he went his way, not whither he would not, but rather whither he would.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And though when set free from the body he wished to be with Christ, yet, were it only possible, he had a desire for eternal life apart from the grievousness of death, to which grievous experience he was unwillingly carried, but from it [when all was over] he was willingly carried away; unwillingly he came to it, but willingly he conquered it, and left this feeling of infirmity behind that makes every one unwilling to die,—a feeling so permanently natural, that even old age itself was unable to set the blessed Peter free from its influence, even as it was said unto him, “When thou shalt be old,” thou shall be led “whither thou wouldest not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For our consolation the Saviour Himself transfigured also the same feeling in His own person when He said, “Father, if it be possible, let this cup pass from me;” and He certainly had come to die without having any necessity, but only the willingness to die, with power to lay down His life, and with power to take it again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But however great be the grievousness of death, it ought to be overcome by the power of that love which is felt to Him who, being our life, was willing to endure even death in our behalf.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if there were no grievousness, even of the smallest kind, in death, the glory of the martyrs would not be so great.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if the good Shepherd, who laid down His own life for His sheep, has raised up so many martyrs for Himself out of the very sheep, how much more ought those to contend to death for the truth, and even to blood against sin, who are entrusted by Him with the feeding, that is, with the teaching and governing of these very sheep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And on this account, along with the preceding example of His own passion, who can fail to see that the shepherds ought all the more to set themselves closely to imitate the Shepherd, if He was so imitated even by many of the sheep under whom, as the one Shepherd and in the one flock, the shepherds themselves are likewise sheep?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He made all those His sheep for [all of] whom He died, because He Himself also became a sheep that He might suffer for all.
